text: The manager said 'I love working weekends'.
note: quoted speech — pattern fires on 'I love' but it's not user preference

text: I don't really prefer vanilla anymore.
I used to love sushi but now I can't stand it.
note: past positive flipped to present negative

text: My friend hates mushrooms, but I love them.
It's not that I don't like coffee, I just prefer tea.
I picked up a new book yesterday.
note: picked = physical action, not choice intent

text: I go to the gym three times a week — it's exhausting.
note: habitual + difficulty co-present — habitual should win on the primary clause

text: The task wasn't hard, just tedious.
She said the app is really intuitive.
I don't hate Django, I just find it verbose.
I would love to switch to Rust, but our stack is all Go.
note: hypothetical preference, actual choice is Go

text: I love how Mongo eventually consistent writes mess up our aggregates.
Patient reports dislike of oral medications.
note: third-person + reported speech; valid clinical preference

text: Patient's family prefers hospice over aggressive treatment.
Considering a switch from warfarin to apixaban.
note: considering = hypothetical choice; classifier may abstain

text: Pain no longer tolerable on current regimen.
I like, I mean I really really really love, rock climbing.
I love hate chocolate — it's complicated.
